question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted stream file that triggers a code path calling setEnabled(false) on the parser, halting all fee-schedule-update transaction processing and causing stale/incorrect custom fees to persist in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publicly mutable setEnabled(boolean) contract on ParserProperties to toggle the record parser off and back on, causing TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions to be skipped and the mirror node to reflect outdated fee schedules that differ from the consensus node state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the parser queue by submitting a burst of TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions that exceed BatchProperties.maxItems (60,000), causing the batch to flush prematurely and persist partial or inconsistent fee schedules to the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file containing exactly BatchProperties.maxItems fee-update records to force a batch boundary at a point where a multi-step fee schedule update is split across two batches, resulting in an intermediate fee state being committed that violates design invariants?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.queueCapacity (default 10) limit by submitting enough concurrent stream files to fill the queue, causing the parser to drop or delay TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions and leaving the mirror node with fees that do not reflect the current on-chain schedule?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.window (default 5 minutes) to delay the processing of a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transaction by up to 5 minutes, during which the mirror node serves stale fee data to downstream consumers who rely on it for fee calculations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file with BatchProperties.maxFiles (default 1) boundary-crossing fee updates such that a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE at the end of one file and a dependent fee read at the start of the next file are processed in separate batches, causing a race condition where fees are read before the update is committed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the minimum frequency constraint (@DurationMin(millis=10L), default 20ms) by timing a burst of TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions to arrive between polling intervals, causing the parser to miss a fee update cycle and serve incorrect fees for up to one polling period?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a high-volume stream of non-fee transactions at the parser's polling frequency (20ms) to saturate the parser thread, delaying TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE processing and causing the mirror node to reflect fees outside the design-intended update latency?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a malformed stream file that causes the parser to hang for the full processingTimeout (default 10s, minimum 5s) while processing a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE, causing the health check to fail and the parser to restart, potentially re-processing or skipping the fee update?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the processingTimeout boundary (@DurationMin(seconds=5)) by crafting a stream file whose fee-update processing consistently takes just under 5 seconds, causing repeated near-timeout conditions that degrade fee update reliability without triggering the health check failure?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file with a mismatched StreamType to exploit the parser's type-routing logic, causing a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transaction to be routed to the wrong parser instance and processed with incorrect fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file that embeds a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transaction inside a BALANCE stream type payload, exploiting the getStreamType() dispatch to bypass fee-schedule validation logic that only runs in the RECORD parser path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.flushInterval (default 2s, minimum 100ms) by submitting fee-update transactions at a rate that keeps the batch perpetually below the flush threshold, preventing TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE records from being committed to the database within the expected time window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between BatchProperties.flushInterval (2s) and BatchProperties.window (5m) to create a scenario where a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE is batched with a large number of other transactions, causing the fee update to be committed with an incorrect consensus timestamp ordering?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the isEnabled() state of the parser through a side channel (e.g., health endpoint or metrics) and time a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE submission to coincide with a parser restart window, causing the fee update to be processed twice or not at all?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the boolean isEnabled() check by submitting a stream file that causes an exception during fee processing, which triggers a retry loop that eventually calls setEnabled(false), permanently disabling fee-schedule-update processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file with BatchProperties.maxItems-1 padding transactions followed by a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE, causing the fee update to be the sole item in a new batch and bypassing any multi-item consistency checks that assume fee updates are processed alongside their associated token transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 20ms default frequency to submit two conflicting TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions within the same polling window, causing the parser to process them in an undefined order and commit a fee schedule that does not match the intended final state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.maxFiles (default 1) constraint by splitting a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE across two stream files, where the first file sets up a fee collector and the second file sets the fee amount, causing the parser to commit an incomplete fee schedule after processing only the first file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file containing a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE with an extremely large custom fee list that causes processing to exceed the processingTimeout (10s), triggering a timeout exception that leaves the fee schedule in a partially updated state in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.queueCapacity (10) by submitting 10 stream files containing TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions simultaneously, causing queue overflow that results in the most recent fee update being dropped and the mirror node reflecting an outdated fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a parser exception during TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE processing that causes the AbstractStreamFileParser to call setEnabled(false) as an error recovery mechanism, effectively disabling all future fee-schedule processing without operator intervention?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between BatchProperties.window (5m) and the parser's catch-up logic to cause a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE to be batched with transactions from a different time window, resulting in the fee update being applied with an incorrect effective timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the minimum frequency constraint (10ms) to cause the parser to poll so rapidly that TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transactions are processed before their associated token creation transactions, resulting in fee schedules being applied to non-existent tokens?
